author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
nijakarma⋅doṣa⋅phale, paḍi’ bhavārṇava⋅jale,
hābuḍubu khāi katakāla
sā̐tāri’ sā̐tāri’ jāi, sindhu⋅anta nāhi pāi,
bhavasindhu ananta viśāla

nimagna hoinu jabe, ḍākinu kātara rabe,
keha more karaha uddhāra
seikāle āile tumi, tomā jāni’ kūlabhūmi,
āśābīja hoila āmāra

tumi hari dayāmaya, pāile more suniścaya,
sarvottama dayāra viṣaya
tomāke nā chāḍi āra, e bhaktivinoda chāra,
dayāpātra pāile dayāmaya
en_translation:
O Hari! As a result of my own faulty deeds, I have fallen into the ocean of material existence. How long will I continue to drown and float? Swimming and swimming, I find no end to this infinitely vast ocean.

When I was submerged, I called out with a distressed cry, "Someone please save me!" At that moment, You came. Knowing You to be my shore, the seed of hope sprouted within me.

You, Hari, are merciful. You have surely found me to be the most deserving subject of Your mercy. I will not leave You anymore; this wretched Bhaktivinoda has found the object of Your compassion, O merciful one!
